sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: SV Zulte Waregem have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, with 2 wins and 3 losses, scoring 6 goals and conceding 9. Royal Antwerp FC are also struggling, with only 1 win, 2 draws, and 2 losses in their last 5, scoring 4 goals and conceding 6. Both teams are in mid-table with 10 points each, indicating a closely matched encounter.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last 2 head-to-head matches, each team has secured 1 win, with low-scoring outcomes (0-1 and 0-2). Historically, matches between these teams often see under 2.5 goals (5 out of 6), and Royal Antwerp FC have been first to score in 4 of the last 5 encounters.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: SV Zulte Waregem have scored 11 goals this season (ranked 6th) but struggle defensively, conceding 13 (ranked 13th). Royal Antwerp FC also have 9 goals scored and conceded, showing a balanced but uninspiring record. Both teams average around 5 corners per game, suggesting moderate attacking intent.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: Zulte Waregem have a slight home advantage at Elindus Arena and have shown late-game resilience (3 goals in 76-90 minutes). Antwerp's recent inability to win (3 games without a victory) and their tendency to score early could be crucial. Yellow cards are likely, given both teams' foul counts and the referee's average of 3 per game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between SV Zulte Waregem and Royal Antwerp FC ended in a goalless draw at the Elindus Arena in Waregem. Neither side managed to break the deadlock, with both teams showing moments of promise but lacking the final touch in front of goal. Zulte Waregem showed more intent in creating significant opportunities, while Antwerp held a slight edge in possession and controlled the game for extended periods. Defensive solidity has been a key feature so far, with both teams well-organized at the back.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating attack momentum between the two sides. Zulte Waregem started strongly with consistent attacking spells in the opening minutes, pushing forward with intent. However, momentum shifted to Antwerp around the midway point of the half as they enjoyed a prolonged period of pressure and looked to dominate the game. Towards the latter stages of the half, Zulte Waregem regained control with several dangerous attacking moves, particularly in the closing minutes before the break. Antwerp responded with some late pressure but couldn't sustain it as the half ended.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Home team performance
halftimeSections.text: SV Zulte Waregem showed promise in the first half, particularly in their ability to create chances in the final third. They registered a notable big chance but failed to convert, highlighting a need for sharper finishing. Their defensive structure has been robust, and they've won a majority of their duels, especially in the air. Players like Anosike Ementa and Jeppe Erenbjerg have been lively, contributing to key attacking plays. If they maintain this energy, they could unlock Antwerp's defense in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Away team performance
halftimeSections.text: Royal Antwerp FC had more possession in the first half but struggled to translate it into meaningful opportunities. They failed to create any big chances, and their final third play lacked precision despite several penalty area entries. Defensively, they've held firm, though their aerial duels and overall duel-winning stats suggest they’ve been second-best in physical battles. Marwan Alsahafi has looked dangerous at times, and Antwerp will need more from players like him to turn possession into goals.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key areas to watch
halftimeSections.text: The second half will likely hinge on which team can capitalize on their attacking entries into the final third. Zulte Waregem's ability to create big chances makes them a threat, but they must be more clinical. Antwerp, on the other hand, needs to improve their creativity in the final third to match their possession dominance with genuine goal-scoring opportunities. Set pieces could play a role, as both teams have had corners but haven't yet made them count. Discipline will also be key, with Antwerp already picking up a yellow card.
